free_text: Stellar s-process neutron capture cross sections on
INAF - Osservatorio Astronomico di Roma, Monte Porzio
Proton beam (1930+/-5 keV with spread of
15 keV in one standard deviation) on liquid Li target
Kr gas in Ti spherical shell (10 mm outer diam.,
Two Au foils (~12.5 um thick) in front of the Kr
ZrO2+Al pellet (~1 mm thick) between the two Au foils
which has negligible effect (~1%) on the neutron
Irradiated for 7.28 mA-h protons at Ep=1930 keV
for (n,g)+(g,n) activation, and also for 11.7 mA-h
protons at Ep=1816 keV for (g,n) activation
The measured 198Au activity agrees with the activity
- gamma-ray self absorption in sample and Ti shell
- variation of neutron yield during irradiation
kT=3 to 300 keV Maxwellian averaged cross sections
Total uncertainty propagated from the partial
uncertainties in Table XI of Phys.Rev.C104(2021)015806
~2.5 hr after the end of irradiation, then six
four-hour gamma counting runs ~1.5 days later.
